leadText: The Oval Charm (Japanese: まるいおまもり Round Charm) is a Key Item introduced in Pokémon Black 2 and White 2. It increases the chance of an Egg being found at Pokémon Day Care or Pokémon Nursery.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Effect [ edit | edit source ] The Oval Charm increases the chance of an Egg being found at Pokémon Day Care or the Pokémon Nursery . The chance varies as follows, depending on what the chance would normally be. (It does not increase the chance of an Egg appearing if Eggs couldn't normally appear.) Species OTs Normal chance Chance with Oval Charm Same Different 70% 88% Same Same 50% 80% Different Different Different Same 20% 40%

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description B2 W2 An oval charm said to increase the chance of Eggs being found at the Day Care. X Y OR AS An oval charm said to increase the chance of Pokémon Eggs being found at the Day Care. S M US UM P E Sw Sh BD SP LA An oval charm said to increase the chance of Pokémon Eggs being found at the Nursery. S V Having one of these mysterious oval charms makes it more likely Pokémon Eggs might be found during picnics.

Acquisition [ edit | edit source ] Games Method B2 W2 Juniper Pokémon Lab (from Professor Juniper for obtaining all the Pokémon in the Unova Pokédex , except Mythical Pokémon ) X Y Sycamore Pokémon Lab (from Professor Sycamore after seeing all Pokémon in each section of the Kalos Pokédex , except Articuno , Zapdos , Moltres , Diancie , Hoopa , and Volcanion ) OR AS Littleroot Town (from Professor Birch after seeing all Pokémon in the Hoenn Pokédex , except Jirachi ) S M US UM Heahea City (from Morimoto after defeating him for the first time) Sw Sh Circhester (from Morimoto after defeating him for the first time) BD SP Valor Lakefront (from Morimoto after defeating him for the first time)

sections.title: Trivia
sections.text: Trivia [ edit | edit source ] Even though this item is not obtainable in Pokémon Scarlet and Violet , its description has been changed to reflect picnics as the new method of breeding.
